articles.title: THE FUTURE OF AI IN 2025
articles.summary: Dive into 23 perspectives with top AI industry leaders [4] like Amin Vahdat (Google Cloud), David Friedberg (Ohalo Genetics), Chamath Palihapitiya (Social Capital), Crystal Huang (GV), Harrison Chase (LangChain), and more—as they share their predictions, real-world examples, and advice to help you position your business for success in 2025 and beyond.

articles.title: AMAZON'S ALEXA+
articles.summary: Amazon has introduced Alexa+, an enhanced version of its voice assistant. Alexa+ is a generative AI-powered assistant that is smarter and more conversational.

articles.title: ELEVENLAB'S SPEECH-TO-TEXT
articles.summary: ElevenLabs is launching its own transcription model, Scribe. It will support 99 languages with high accuracy, word-level timestamps, speaker diarization, and real-world audio adaptability.

articles.title: GROK 3 APPEARS TO HAVE BRIEFLY CENSORED UNFLATTERING MENTIONS OF TRUMP AND MUSK
articles.summary: Elon Musk's Grok 3 AI model briefly censored mentions of Donald Trump and Musk in misinformation queries but reverted after user feedback. xAI's engineering lead confirmed an employee made the change with good intentions, but it was not aligned with company values. Musk aims to make Grok politically neutral after previous models showed left-leaning tendencies.

articles.title: QWQ MAX PREVIEW
articles.summary: Qwen has previewed a reasoning model that achieves strong results in math and code. It intends to open-weight release this model along with its powerful Max model.

articles.title: LOCAL MODELS AS MINIONS
articles.summary: Hazy Research has found that if you use local models via Ollama and use a long context cloud model as the orchestrator, you can achieve 97% task performance at 17% of the cost.

articles.title: SYSTEM 2 THINKING IN LLMS
articles.summary: A survey on reasoning LLMs like OpenAI's o1/o3 and DeepSeek's R1 that analyzes their step-by-step logical reasoning capabilities and benchmarks their performance against human cognitive abilities.

articles.title: OLMOCR FOR PDF TEXT EXTRACTION
articles.summary: Allen AI has trained a strong extraction model for PDFs by continued fine tuning of Qwen VL on 200k+ PDFs.

articles.title: AI SAFETY EVALUATION
articles.summary: AISafetyLab is a comprehensive AI safety framework that covers attack, defense, and evaluation. It includes models, datasets, utilities, and a curated list of AI safety-related papers.

articles.title: PUBLIC OPINION PREDICTION WITH SURVEY-BASED FINE-TUNING
articles.summary: SubPOP introduces a large dataset for fine-tuning LLMs to predict survey response distributions, reducing prediction gaps and improving generalization to unseen surveys.

articles.title: MAGMA: A FOUNDATION MODEL FOR MULTIMODAL AI AGENTS (HUGGING FACE HUB)
articles.summary: Magma is a new foundation model for visual agent tasks and excels at video understanding and UI navigation. It is easy to tune.

articles.title: CLAUDE AI POWERS ALEXA+
articles.summary: Anthropic's Claude AI is now integrated into Alexa+ via Amazon Bedrock, enhancing its capabilities while maintaining strong safety protections against jailbreaking and misuse.

articles.title: MICROSOFT RELEASES NEW PHI MODELS OPTIMIZED FOR MULTIMODAL PROCESSING
articles.summary: Microsoft has released two new open-source language models, Phi-4-mini and Phi-4-multimodal, emphasizing hardware efficiency and multimodal processing. Phi-4-mini, which has 3.8 billion parameters, focuses on text tasks, while Phi-4-multimodal, with 5.6 billion parameters, processes text, images, audio, and video. Both models outperform similar-sized alternatives and will be available on Hugging Face under an MIT license.

articles.title: CHARTA HEALTH RAISES $8.1 MILLION
articles.summary: Charta Health raised $8.1M led by Bain Capital Ventures to enhance AI-driven pre-bill chart reviews, reducing billing errors and recapturing lost revenue.

articles.title: FLORA LAUNCHES CURSOR FOR CREATIVES
articles.summary: FLORA is the first AI-powered creative workflow tool built for creative professionals to 10x their creative output.

articles.title: GOOGLE'S NEW AI VIDEO MODEL VEO 2 WILL COST 50 CENTS PER SECOND
articles.summary: Google's Veo 2 AI video model costs $0.50 per second, translating to $30 per minute.
